channel.title: Apache Software Foundation Events
channel.description: Recent content on Apache Software Foundation Events

item.description: CoC Glasgow Hackathon — New Responses Log Responses from projects replying to the hackathon outreach emails. Newest entries at top. 2026-05-12 - Apache httpd (Jean-Frédéric Clere) From: Jean-Frédéric Clere jfclere@gmail.com Summary: Replied privately (directly to Rich, not to the dev list) to the httpd hackathon outreach. Note: full message content is in Rich’s inbox (Mac Mail ID: 722274). Needs follow-up to determine interest level and whether Jean-Frédéric can serve as PoC.
2026 Events Community over Code Glasgow — October 11–14, Glasgow, UK

item.title: About Apache Roadshows
item.description: Apache Roadshows are small regional one- or two-day events, intended to build local community and educate about what the Apache Software Foundation does. While not a traditional roadshow (ie, a traveling event with multiple stops), we use the name to indicate that there may be multiple small events on a particular continent. Thus, the Europe Roadshow may have small events in Berlin and Barcelona during the course of a year, while the North America Roadshow might have events in DC and Chicago.

item.title: About ASF Events
item.description: The Apache Software Foundation (ASF) runs, or participates in some fashion in, a number of different types of events, which different target audiences and goals, to educate about our projects and about the Foundation, and to build the communities. The main types of event we work with around ASF projects are: Community Over Code - the ASF’s official conference Endorsed events - run by third parties using Apache brands If you want to organize your own event that uses ASF brands or trademarks in any way, we strongly encourage you to coordinate with us.

item.title: About Community Over Code
item.description: Community Over Code (formerly ApacheCon) is the official conference of the Apache Software Foundation. Its primary goals are education and community building. We try to produce one each calendar year somewhere in North America and Europe, and are working on expanding this to the rest of the world. If you want to volunteer to help with Community Over Code, please join our mailing lists, and see what volunteer opportunities we have, bearing in mind that it sometimes gets out of date.

item.title: Apache Conferences and Events Mailing Lists
item.description: There are several main mailing lists on which discussion and planning of Apache Software Foundation events occurs: ApacheCon Announce announce@apachecon.com Subscription address: announce-subscribe@apachecon.com Archives: Web archives Use: Read-only: Announcements of, and details about, Apache events Community Development dev@community.apache.org Subscription address: dev-subscribe@community.apache.org Archives: Web archives Use: Broader community development activies across the ASF. See the comdev site for more details. Planners (Planning committee members only) planners@apachecon.com Subscription address: planners-subscribe@apachecon.com Subscription note: All subscriptions are moderated.

item.title: Apache Conferences Calendar
item.description: Upcoming Apache events This is the list of upcoming official Apache events, and those events which have been approved to use Apache Marks under the Third Party Event Branding Policy. Sorry, this requires Javascript For a complete list of events see our calendar. In addition to Official Apache Events, there are a wide range of other events which feature Apache projects and technologies.

item.title: Apache Conferences Calendar
Apache Endorsed Events
item.description: Apache endorsed/approved events There are many third-party events produced every year that focus on Apache Software Foundation projects. The ASF encourages these events, as they expand the reach of our projects and our message. If you are producing an Apache-related event, and using our brands and marks, we ask that contact us for our official approval. Along with this, we will also help promote these events, where possible and appropriate. Whether or not you want to collaborate with us on your event, you MUST adhere to our branding policy.

item.title: Apache Past Events
item.description: Past ApacheCons For details on past ApacheCons, and archived sites of previous events (where available), please see https://archive.apachecon.com/ Information on past ApacheCons is also available on the ApacheCon Wiki

item.title: Apache Tomcat — Hackathon at Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Register now | Event website | Hackathon overview Apache Tomcat — Hackathon Coordinator Name — markt@apache.org What We’re Working On We will be working on new features and enhancements to Apache Tomcat. The ideas list is being maintained on the project Wiki. We welcome additional suggestions on the dev@ list if you have an idea for something you’d like to work on.

item.title: Apache YourProject — Hackathon at Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Register now | Event website | Hackathon overview Apache YourProject — Hackathon Coordinator Name — email@apache.org What We’re Working On We’re focusing on: Good first issues — small, well-scoped tasks suitable for new contributors: GitHub issues labeled “good first issue” Documentation improvements — help us improve our getting-started guides and API docs Bug fixes — a curated set of bugs that are approachable with mentoring support Resources Contribution guide Dev mailing list thread about the hackathon GitHub issues Slack / Discord / chat channel Dev environment setup Who’s Planning to Attend Name (role or area of expertise) Name (role or area of expertise) Getting Started Before the Event To make the most of hackathon time, we recommend:

item.title: ApacheCon playbook
item.description: This playbook is focused at ApacheCon or Roadshow volunteers who are actively involved in planning and running Apache events. Please be sure to bring your questions to the planners@ mailing list! What to do before, during, and after an Apache event This is a set of recommended actions that an event organizer may do before, during, and after ApacheCon. Many of the recommendations in here can, of course, be applied to any Apache event or Roadshow, including third-party events, or project events run by PMCs.

item.title: Call For Volunteers, Community over Code 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 will be held in Glasgow, UK, October 11-14. Community over Code is an event run by the community, for the community. That’s you! We need your help to make it a success! We are looking for volunteers for a number of community engagement tasks. These roles include: Hackathon Coordination - Help organize and run the hackathon University Outreach - Connect with colleges and universities across multiple departments Meetup Outreach - Engage with local meetup groups Speaker Support - Provide coaching and feedback to speakers Design - Create visual materials for outreach and on-site If you are interested in volunteering for any of these jobs, please:

item.title: Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Community over Code is the annual conference of the Apache Software Foundation — bringing together developers, community managers, and enthusiasts from across the ASF ecosystem. Get Involved Hackathon — Dedicated time and space for contributors to work together on real project tasks. 25 projects are already signed up. Call for Volunteers — Help make the event a success — hackathon coordination, university outreach, meetup engagement, speaker support, and design.

item.title: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - Hackathon Outreach Calendar
item.description: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - October 11-14, Hilton Glasgow Want to help promote the hackathon? Grab a ready-made post from our Social Media Promotion page and share it wherever you hang out online. Copy, paste, tweak, post! Conference dates: October 11 - 14, 2026 - Glasgow, Scotland This page tracks the outreach tasks leading up to the hackathon, and follow-up tasks afterward. Each task needs a volunteer owner. Click “Volunteer for this task!

item.title: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - Volunteer Outreach Calendar
item.description: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - October 11-14, Hilton Glasgow Conference dates: October 11-14, 2026 - Glasgow, Scotland This page tracks volunteer outreach tasks for Community over Code Glasgow - speaker support, local community engagement, attendee experience, and post-event follow-up. Each task needs a volunteer owner. NOTE: Speaker outreach tasks require coordination through Brian Proffitt and the track chairs. Speaker contact information is not publicly shared. Outreach resources: Glasgow Schools & Universities Glasgow Diversity & Inclusion Groups Glasgow Tech Meetups Glasgow guides (help us improve these!

item.title: Day-of Checklist
item.description: Event Day Checklist Print this and bring it with you. Check items off as you go. Before doors open (arrive 30–60 min early) Unlock the room / meet the venue contact Set up tables and chairs in the arrangement you want Test the WiFi — connect a device, run a speed test, do a git clone Test the projector / screen if using one Put up directional signs from building entrance to room Write on whiteboard or print on paper: WiFi network name and password Project chat channel (Slack/Discord/IRC) Event hashtag Schedule overview Set out power strips / extension cords Set out any swag (stickers, name badges) If catering: confirm delivery time and dietary accommodations As people arrive Greet people, point them to WiFi info Have them add their name to an attendance list (paper or shared doc) If you’re doing name badges, hand them out or let people write their own Help anyone who needs dev environment setup Event start Brief welcome (5 min max): What we’re doing today Where to find issues/tasks How to ask questions (who’s available to help) Logistics: food, bathrooms, end time Code of Conduct reminder (link: apache.

item.title: Design Volunteers
item.description: Visual design plays a crucial role in promoting Apache events and reaching diverse audiences. Design volunteers create materials that help communicate the value and accessibility of Apache events. Design Opportunities Posters and Print Materials Event posters and landing pages for different outreach audiences Flyers for university and meetup distribution Design Considerations Maintain consistency with Apache brand guidelines Create inclusive, welcoming visuals Design for accessibility Consider diverse audiences and contexts Support multiple formats and sizes Skills Needed Graphic design experience Understanding of web design principles Attention to accessibility standards Get Involved If you have design skills and want to help promote Apache events, please come talk to us!

item.title: Events
item.description: Current Events Community over Code Glasgow 2026 — October 11–14, Glasgow, UK Coordinate Your Community Event Here This site is available to all ASF project communities to organize and promote meetups, hackathons, sprints, release parties, or any community gathering. Whether you’re planning something local or virtual, we can help you get the word out. To get started: Read the How to Organize a Small Apache Event guide Copy one of our event page templates Submit a PR to this repo with your event page Send a note to dev@community.

item.title: Getting Involved in Apache Events
item.description: There are a number of different ways to get involved with Events at Apache. Many of these come under the umbrella of the Conferences Committee, but some fall under different groups, such as the Apache Community Development Project. If you are an Apache Committer, and you’re interested in putting on an Apache Event, please see the Organising Events section for more help. If you are interested in helping others out with putting on an Apache Event, you are advised to join the appropriate mailing list, and volunteer for events there.

item.title: Glasgow Bars & Pubs
item.description: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - October 11-14, Hilton Glasgow A guide to bars, pubs, and nightlife near the Community over Code Glasgow venue (Hilton Glasgow, 1 William St, city centre). Glasgow has a legendary pub culture. Here are some highlights within walking distance of the conference venue. Good Beer Guide Pubs (CAMRA-recommended) Babbity Bowster What: Tucked-away pub-restaurant-hotel in Merchant City with a reputation for excellent beer, wine, and food.

item.title: Glasgow Diversity & Inclusion Groups - Outreach Contacts
item.description: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - October 11-14, Hilton Glasgow This page lists Glasgow-area diversity-in-tech groups and organizations. We’re inviting their members to attend and participate in Community over Code Glasgow (October 11-14, 2026) and the hackathon. Diversity-in-Tech Organizations Scotland Women in Technology (SWiT) Website: https://www.scotwomenintech.com/ About: Scotland-wide organization supporting women in technology. Runs annual awards, events, and networking. Relevance: Established community with engaged members who’d benefit from conference exposure.

item.title: Glasgow Schools & Universities - Outreach Contacts
item.description: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - October 11-14, Hilton Glasgow This page lists Glasgow-area educational institutions with computing/technology programmes. We’re inviting students and faculty to attend and participate in the hackathon at Community over Code Glasgow (October 11-14, 2026). If you’re volunteering for student outreach, use this list as your starting point. Offer free or discounted student passes and highlight the hackathon as a hands-on learning opportunity. Universities University of Glasgow - School of Computing Science Website: https://www.

item.title: Glasgow Tech Meetups - Outreach Contacts
item.description: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - October 11-14, Hilton Glasgow This page lists Glasgow-area tech meetups and developer communities. We’re inviting their members to attend Community over Code Glasgow (October 11-14, 2026) and participate in the hackathon. Active Glasgow Tech Meetups Python Glasgow Website: https://www.meetup.com/python-glasgow/ Schedule: 1st Wednesday of every month About: Monthly meetup for sharing perspectives and insights about Python. Hosts speakers from across the city. Venue: The Gamer Club Relevance: Python is central to many Apache projects.

item.title: Glasgow Tourist Highlights
item.description: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - October 11-14, Hilton Glasgow Attending Community over Code Glasgow? Here are the top things to see and do in Glasgow beyond the conference. Most are free and within easy reach of the venue. Top Free Attractions Kelvingrove Art Gallery and Museum What: World-class art and natural history museum in a stunning red sandstone building. 22 galleries, everything from Dali to dinosaurs. Where: Argyle Street, Glasgow G3 8AG (map) Cost: Free Time needed: 2-3 hours Glasgow Cathedral What: Medieval cathedral dating to the 12th century - one of the few Scottish medieval churches to survive the Reformation intact Where: Castle Street, Glasgow G4 0QZ (map) Cost: Free Time needed: 45 min - 1 hour Glasgow Necropolis What: Atmospheric Victorian cemetery on a hill behind the Cathedral.

item.title: Glasgow Vegetarian & Vegan Restaurants
item.description: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - October 11-14, Hilton Glasgow A guide to vegetarian and vegan-friendly dining near the Community over Code Glasgow venue (Hilton Glasgow, 1 William St, city centre). Glasgow was named Scotland’s most veg-friendly city, and you’ll find excellent plant-based options all within walking distance of the venue. Fully Vegan Restaurants The Glasvegan What: Plant-based fast food - burgers, hot dogs, loaded fries Where: 50 St Enoch Square, Glasgow G1 4DH (map) Good for: Quick, filling, affordable Mono What: Vegan cafe/bar in Merchant City with an in-house record store and live music Where: 12 Kings Court, King Street, Glasgow G1 5RB (map) Good for: Casual meals, evening hangouts, salads, pizzas, sandwiches The Hug and Pint What: Vegan food above a live music venue Where: 171 Great Western Road, Glasgow G4 9AW (map) Good for: Evening meals before/after a gig, creative Asian-inspired dishes Rose & Grants What: Fully vegan cafe, open 7 days for breakfast and lunch Where: 27 Trongate, Glasgow G1 5EZ (map) Good for: Breakfast/brunch.

item.title: Hackathon at Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Community over Code 2026 — Glasgow, UK, October 11–14 Hackathon Hackathon We’re bringing back the hackathon! Community over Code Glasgow will include dedicated time and space for contributors — new and experienced — to sit together and work on real tasks: fixing bugs, writing docs, tackling issues that need focused attention, and onboarding new contributors. How it works Each participating project provides:

item.title: Hackathon Coordination
item.description: Hackathons are hands-on collaborative events where developers work together on Apache projects. A hackathon coordinator works with representatives from projects likely to have a presence at event to help them think about what they might work on, and publicize this work to the project community and the audience of the event. They’ll also work with the planners of the event to create a space with the necessary components (tables, power outlets, signage, etc) to make that space welcoming to participants, including beginners.

item.title: Help Promote Community over Code Glasgow 2026
item.description: Community over Code Glasgow 2026 - October 11-14, Hilton Glasgow Help Get the Word Out! You don’t need to be a “social media person” to help promote this event. Just pick a post below, customize it if you like, and share it on whatever platform you use. Every share helps. Copy, paste, tweak, post. That’s it. Hashtags: #CommunityOverCode #ASF2026Glasgow #OpenSource Conference website: https://communityovercode.org/ This Week’s Suggested Post Updated weekly - check back for fresh content!

item.title: How to Organize a Small Apache Event
item.description: How to Organize a Small Apache Event This guide covers everything you need to run a local event — a hackathon, sprint, meetup, BarCamp, or community gathering — with Apache branding. You don’t need permission from ComDev to start planning; just follow the steps below. What counts as a “small event”? Any community gathering of 5–100 people organized by or for an Apache project community: Contributor sprint — a day of focused hacking on a specific project Hackathon — multi-project working event BarCamp / unconference — participant-driven talks and discussion Meetup — an evening or half-day of talks and networking Release party — celebrating a major release Step-by-step 1.

item.title: Meetup Outreach
item.description: Reaching out to local meetup groups is a great way to get a larger audience to our events, and serve the local technical community. As a meetup outreach coordinator, you’ll be identifying those meetup groups, and getting the word out to them. Volunteers should be local to the event venue. What Meetup Outreach Involves Identify relevant local meetup groups Build relationships with local tech communities Look for related topics, like legal, business, and communication, which may be interested in open source and Apache projects Share information about Apache events with meetup communities Encourage meetup participants to submit talks for the upcoming event Types of Meetup Groups you might conenct with We can engage with various types of groups:

item.title: Organize an Apache Event
item.description: Organize an Apache Event Whether you’re planning a small contributor sprint or a multi-day BarCamp, this section has everything you need to get started. Small Community Events (NEW) Most Apache events are organized by individual contributors in their local communities. You don’t need special permission — just follow our self-service guide: How to Organize a Small Apache Event — Step-by-step process from idea to execution Sprint in a Box — Everything for a one-day coding sprint (the simplest possible event) Day-of Checklist — Printable logistics checklist Templates To create your event page, make a new directory under source/events/YYYY/your-event-name/ in this repo and copy one of these templates as your starting __index.

item.title: Roadshow Bid Process
item.description: DRAFT This is intended to document the bid process for running/hosting a Roadshow. In particular, if you want ASF budget for it. You need, at a minimum Event chair Must be an ASF member Must be resident in the city where the event is proposed Volunteers to support (how many?) City/Venue selected University cities are preferred Local tech firms that can sponsor? Local keynote suggestion Proposed timeline, starting with CFP, going through schedule, speaker notification, the event, and the post-event Budget Leads on sponsorship helpful Contribute to event “runbook” as output from the event To run a roadshow or other small event without ASF budget, you only need to:

item.title: Speaker Support and Coaching
item.description: Great presentations help spread knowledge about Apache projects and the ASF. Our speaker support volunteers provide coaching and feedback to help speakers deliver engaging, effective talks. What speaker support volunteers do As a volunteer, you’ll offer several types of speaker support: CFP and Abstract Support Review and provide feedback on conference proposals Help refine talk titles and abstracts Advise on targeting proposals to specific audiences Suggest improvements to increase acceptance chances Slide Review and Editing Review presentation slides for clarity and flow Provide feedback on visual design and readability Suggest improvements to technical content Help ensure slides align with talk objectives and ASF branding Presentation Rehearsal Conduct dress rehearsal sessions via video chat Provide constructive feedback on delivery Help with timing and pacing Offer tips on handling questions Build speaker confidence Who Can Help We welcome volunteers with:

item.title: Sprint in a Box
item.description: Sprint in a Box Everything you need to run a one-day contributor sprint for your Apache project. This is the simplest possible event — no talks, no sponsors needed, just a room and people who want to code together. Minimum requirements Room with tables, chairs, WiFi, and power outlets for 5–30 people One coordinator who is a committer on the project 3+ committed attendees before you set a date 4–6 weeks lead time for promotion Timeline 6 weeks before Confirm room (university, office, coworking space, library) Set up event page using the event template Post to your project’s dev@ list: “Who’s interested?

item.title: University and Educational Outreach
item.description: Apache projects offer valuable learning opportunities for students and educators across many disciplines. University outreach volunteers help connect Apache communities with educational institutions at all levels. Outreach Opportunities University outreach volunteers should be local to the event venue, and familiar with local educational institutions. We engage with multiple departments and programs: Computer Science - Software development, open source practices, distributed systems Legal Studies - Open source licensing, intellectual property, foundation governance Business - Community management, project governance, open source business models Design - User experience, visual design, accessibility Other Disciplines - Technical writing, marketing, communications, and more What Outreach Volunteers Do Connect with faculty and student organizations Create simple posters targeted at each audience Create more detailed landing pages for each audience, describing what they might get out of the event.

item.title: Volunteer Opportunities
item.description: The Apache Software Foundation events program relies on volunteers to help make our events successful and welcoming. Whether you have a few hours or want to take on a larger role, there are many ways to contribute. We welcome volunteers of all experience levels and backgrounds! You do not need to be an Apache member, committer, or an expert on any particular Apache project to volunteer for these task. Join the #volunteers channel on the apachecon.

item.title: Volunteer to help at ApacheCon
item.description: Volunteer to help at ApacheCon Coordination of ApacheCon happens on two lists: The private planners@apachecon.com mailing list for most of the planning work The public apachecon-discuss@apache.org mailing list for general discussion, calls for volunteers, announcements, and so on. At this time, we can use help in the following areas - typically for each ApacheCon that happens. To volunteer, please send email to the apachecon-discuss mailing list indicating your availability. Calls For Papers The CFP for each ApacheCon draws hundreds of submissions about projects all around the ASF.
